attestation.statement: Clinician (clinical sign-off authority) confirmed having clinically reviewed all 50 DST (Dermatology & Soft Tissue) answer keys in this batch and attests them clinically correct, and authorised ingest. Bidirectional tier calibration reviewed and accepted: benign / self-limiting dermatoses held low-tier (T1/T2, self-care + routine referral), while skin malignancy (melanoma/SCC/BCC), spreading soft-tissue infection, limb-threat (critical limb ischaemia, diabetic foot ulcer) and airway/anaphylaxis risk were escalated to true acuity (T3/T4) with mandatory escalation edges; no autonomous diagnosis, no paediatric dosing.
attestation.recorded_by: claude-opus-4-8 (agent, on clinician's explicit written confirmation in-session)
attestation.scope: all DST casebundles in this batch (n=50)

transform_flags: Provisional case_id SPEC-DERM-03-00236 - SEQ is provisional; maintainer/ingest --reseq to confirm/reassign within the SPEC-DERM-03 bucket.

Age NOT stated in the source note; inferred as an adult (42 y, adult age band) from employment, a years-long chronic dandruff history and a stated 78 kg body weight - clinician to confirm/correct; no bearing on management.
Sex at birth NOT stated in the source note; assigned 'male' solely for schema conformance (demographics.sex_at_birth is required) - no bearing on management; clinician to confirm/correct.

Telehealth reprojection applied: the source is an in-person out-of-hours residential visit with a full clinician examination. Clinician-only findings (mildly injected conjunctivae, intact extraocular movements, grossly normal visual acuity, absence of vesicles/punched-out erosions/spreading cellulitic change, measured vitals BP 132/84 HR 90 Temp 36.8 RR 16 SpO2 99% Wt 78 kg) are sealed in 10/11 telehealth_limits; patient/video-observable data (greasy-scale distribution, eyelid crusting/stuck-shut eyes, self-reported normal vision, no fever) placed in 01.objective_data_offered.

Tiering: correct_baseline_tier T2 (prompt in-person GP eye look within 24-48 h) with minimum_viable_tier_for_pass T1 - a deliberate over-triage-resistance calibration. The alarming periocular flare and severe distress pull toward ED/antibiotics, but the patient-obtainable sight-threat and systemic red-flag screen is NEGATIVE, so the correct answer is recognise-benign + topical treatment + lid hygiene + mandatory eye safety-net + prompt in-person eye look, NOT baseline ED escalation or systemic antibiotics/antivirals.

SNOMED CT-AU / ICD-10-AM codes are candidates from model knowledge (seborrhoeic dermatitis 50563003 / L21.9; blepharitis 41446000 / H01.0; cellulitis 128045006; eczema herpeticum 240593004; psoriasis 9014002; rosacea 398909004) - all unverified pending terminology receipt. AMT codes for ketoconazole 2% shampoo and hydrocortisone 1% cream omitted rather than fabricated.

No dose synthesised beyond what the source note specifies (ketoconazole 2% shampoo every 2-3 days, 5-minute contact; hydrocortisone 1% cream BD for max 5-7 days). Adult patient - no paediatric dosing issue.

difficulty_tier set to red_herring_laden: the explosive periocular flare with intense burning and severe psychosocial distress is a red herring for acute periocular infection; the reasoning task is over-triage/over-treatment resistance with a mandatory eye safety-net.

ingest.hashing: SHA-256 over canonical (JSON 2-space + trailing newline) bytes of each split node
